questions.q: Who founded the film company AVM Productions?
questions.explanation: AVM Productions was founded by A. V. Meiyappan, whose initials give the studio its name.

questions.q: In which Indian city is AVM Productions and its studio based?
questions.explanation: AVM Productions is based in Chennai (formerly Madras), where its studio is located in the Vadapalani area.

questions.q: AVM Productions is often described as which of the following in Indian cinema?
options: The first sound studio in India
The first Technicolor studio in India
The oldest surviving studio in India
The only government-owned studio in India
questions.explanation: Established in 1947, AVM Productions is regarded as the oldest surviving film studio in India.

questions.q: What does the letter 'M' stand for in the name AVM Productions?
questions.explanation: The name AVM comes from the initials of its founder, A. V. Meiyappan.

questions.q: In which year was AVM Productions established under that name?
questions.explanation: AVM Productions was established under that name in 1947.

questions.q: In which languages did AVM Productions make films over its history?
options: Tamil, Telugu, Kannada and Hindi among others
questions.explanation: AVM Productions made films in multiple languages including Tamil, Telugu, Kannada and Hindi.

questions.q: Which Chennai neighbourhood is home to the AVM Studios?
questions.explanation: AVM Studios is located in Vadapalani, Chennai.

questions.q: The founder of AVM Productions, A. V. Meiyappan, began his early business career in which trade before films?
questions.explanation: A. V. Meiyappan started out in the gramophone record business before moving into film production.

questions.q: AVM Productions is best known as a producer of films in which industry first and foremost?
questions.explanation: AVM Productions is primarily associated with Tamil cinema, though it also produced films in other languages.

questions.q: The AVM studio compound in Chennai has historically been used for which purpose besides shooting films?
options: A film recording and post-production facility
questions.explanation: The AVM complex housed shooting floors along with sound recording and post-production facilities.

questions.q: Which 1947 Tamil film was the first film produced by AVM Productions under its own name?
questions.explanation: Naam Iruvar (1947) was the first film made by AVM Productions under the AVM banner.

questions.q: The Hindi children's film Hum Panchhi Ek Daal Ke, which won the first National Film Award for Best Children's Film, was produced by which studio?
questions.explanation: AVM Productions made the children's film Hum Panchhi Ek Daal Ke (1957), which won the inaugural National Film Award for Best Children's Film.

questions.q: Which actress, later Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu, acted in films during the era when AVM Productions was a leading studio early in her film career?
questions.explanation: J. Jayalalithaa, later Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu, was a leading South Indian actress before entering politics.

questions.q: The 1953 AVM Hindi film Ladki and many of the studio's Hindi ventures were aimed at expanding AVM into which market?
questions.explanation: AVM produced Hindi films to extend its reach into the pan-India Hindi-speaking market.

questions.q: Which legendary South Indian filmmaker worked as a director at AVM and later founded his own studio, Prasad Studios?
questions.explanation: L. V. Prasad directed films associated with AVM before establishing Prasad Productions and Prasad Studios.

questions.q: AVM's Tamil film Kalathur Kannamma (1960) is especially remembered for introducing which future superstar as a child actor?
questions.explanation: Kalathur Kannamma (1960), an AVM production, marked the screen debut of Kamal Haasan as a child artist.

questions.q: AVM Productions produced the Tamil film Murattu Kaalai (1980), which was a hit for which leading actor?
questions.explanation: Murattu Kaalai (1980), produced by AVM, was a major success starring Rajinikanth.

questions.q: The 1982 AVM Tamil film Sakalakala Vallavan was a major commercial hit starring which actor?
questions.explanation: Sakalakala Vallavan (1982) was an AVM production starring Kamal Haasan that was a big commercial success.

questions.q: AVM Productions is run as a family enterprise; the studio has been carried forward by which family across generations?
questions.explanation: AVM has remained a family-run studio carried on by the descendants of A. V. Meiyappan.

questions.q: The 2008 Tamil film Vaaranam Aayiram was an AVM production directed by which filmmaker?
questions.explanation: Vaaranam Aayiram (2008), produced by AVM, was directed by Gautham Vasudev Menon.

questions.q: The AVM studio in Chennai is widely regarded as a heritage landmark primarily because of which distinction?
options: It is the oldest surviving film studio in India
It was the first studio built outside India
It was the first to be fully government-funded
It only ever made silent films
questions.explanation: AVM Studios is celebrated as the oldest surviving film studio in India.

questions.q: A. V. Meiyappan, founder of AVM, was honoured by the Indian government with which civilian award?
questions.explanation: A. V. Meiyappan was awarded the Padma Shri for his contribution to Indian cinema.

questions.q: Before adopting the name AVM Productions, A. V. Meiyappan's earliest film venture operated under which banner?
questions.explanation: Meiyappan's early film work was under the Saraswathi Sound Productions banner before AVM was formed.

questions.q: In which decade did A. V. Meiyappan move from the gramophone record business into producing films?
questions.explanation: A. V. Meiyappan moved from the gramophone record trade into film production during the 1930s.

questions.q: Which 1954 AVM Tamil-Telugu bilingual social drama, focusing on the status and emancipation of women, was among the studio's acclaimed early films?
questions.explanation: Penn (1954), an AVM production, was a notable social drama of the studio's early period dealing with women's emancipation.

questions.q: AVM Productions entered Hindi cinema partly through remakes; the 1957 Hindi film Bhabhi produced by AVM was directed by which filmmaker?
questions.explanation: Bhabhi (1957), an AVM Hindi production, was directed by M. V. Raman.

questions.q: Which veteran music director scored many of AVM's early Tamil films and was closely associated with the studio's golden-age sound?
questions.explanation: R. Sudarsanam was a music director long associated with AVM's early Tamil productions.

questions.q: The AVM-produced Hindi film Main Chup Rahungi (1962) was based on the story of which earlier AVM Tamil-language success?
questions.explanation: Main Chup Rahungi (1962) was AVM's Hindi film based on the story of its Tamil hit Kalathur Kannamma.

questions.q: Which child actor made his screen debut in AVM's Kalathur Kannamma (1960) and later became one of India's most acclaimed actors?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan made his debut as a child artist in AVM's Kalathur Kannamma (1960).

questions.q: AVM Productions' founder A. V. Meiyappan hailed from which town in Tamil Nadu, with which the family name is associated?
questions.explanation: A. V. Meiyappan came from the Chettinad region around Karaikudi in Tamil Nadu.
